extra.reasoning: The monotonicity property is violated by the interaction_drop rule, which has the highest priority (100) and forces acuity to self_care (the lowest level) precisely when leg_swelling is true (alongside fever and dyspnea). Take the baseline fever=true, dyspnea=true, leg_swelling=false, all others false: interaction_drop does not match (needs leg_swelling), so the matching rules are raise_dyspnea (pri 3 -> emergent) and raise_fever (pri 2 -> urgent); the highest-priority match is raise_dyspnea, giving acuity=emergent. Now set leg_swelling=true with everything else unchanged: interaction_drop (pri 100) now matches and dominates all the raise_* rules (pri 2-3), forcing acuity=self_care. Acuity drops from emergent to self_care, so setting leg_swelling true lowered acuity, violating the property. The reported witness_assignment is the post-change input (leg_swelling=true) that yields self_care while its leg_swelling=false counterpart yields emergent.
